# Step 5 — Edit Purchase Request (Draft)

> The Creator can edit a PR only when its status is **Draft** (or **Returned**). The form is identical to the Create form but has existing data pre-populated. Changes are saved in-place without creating a new PR.

![Edit PR Form — with item row](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## 5.1 Access Path

- **New PR (not yet saved):** `/procurement/purchase-request/new`
- **Existing Draft:** `/procurement/purchase-request/:uuid` — form is editable when status = Draft
- From PR List: click a Draft PR's row → opens at UUID URL in edit mode
- From PR Detail (Draft): page is already in edit mode; no separate "Edit" button required

---

## 5.2 Flow Position

```mermaid
graph TD
    list["1. PR List"] --> create["2. Create PR"]
    list --> tmpl["3. Create from Template"]
    create --> detail["4. PR Detail"]
    tmpl --> detail
    detail -->|Edit| edit["5. Edit PR"]
    edit --> submit["6. Submit Confirmation"]
    submit --> inprog(["Submitted - In Progress"])
    inprog -->|Returned| returned["7. Returned PR"]
    returned --> edit
    detail -->|Delete| del["8. Delete Confirmation"]
    del --> list
    style edit fill:#1a56db,color:#fff,stroke:#1a56db
```

---

## 5.3 Header Fields

All header fields are the same as the Create form. In Edit mode, Workflow type and Description remain editable; auto-populated fields (Date, Requestor, Department) remain read-only.

| Field | Description | Mandatory | Editable | Pre-populated With |
|-------|-------------|-----------|----------|--------------------|
| PR No. | Auto-generated reference number — assigned on first save with items | — | No | Assigned on first save (blank while at `/new`) |
| Date | PR creation date — set automatically when the PR was first created | — | No | PR creation date |
| Requestor | Full name of the Creator who raised this PR | — | No | Logged-in user name |
| Department | The Creator's department — used for budget tracking and approval routing | — | No | User's department |
| **Workflow** (PR Type) | Determines the approval chain (General / Asset / Market List) | Y | **Yes** | Previously selected type |
| **Description** | Brief explanation of why this PR was raised — visible to all approvers | N | **Yes** | Previously entered text |

---

## 5.4 Items Grid — Edit Mode

Each existing item appears as an inline-editable row. The Creator can change any field or delete the row.

| Column | Description | Mandatory | Type | Default Value | Validation | Business Logic |
|--------|-------------|-----------|------|---------------|------------|----------------|
| **Location** | Where this item will be used or stored within the property | Y | Dropdown | Last used location | Location is not empty | Sub-location (e.g. Main Kitchen). Scoped to the property. |
| **Product** | The item being requested — search by name or code from the inventory master | Y | Searchable dropdown | — (blank) | Product is not empty | Auto-fills Unit and suggests last price. |
| **Requested** | Number of units needed; supports up to 3 decimal places for weighted items | Y | Number | — (blank) | Qty is more than 0; at most 3 decimal places | Requested quantity |
| **Amount** | Total cost = Qty × Unit Price — calculated automatically | Auto | Calculated | 0.00 | Read-only | Updates on quantity or price change |
| **Currency** | Currency for this line item — defaults to the property's base currency | Auto | Dropdown | Base currency (THB) | Defaults to base currency | Override allowed (e.g. THB → USD) |
| **Delivery Point** | Specific room or store within the property for delivery | N | Dropdown (lookup) | Last used delivery point for selected Location | — | Options filtered by selected Location; defaults to last used delivery point for that Location |
| **Delivery Date** | Date the item is needed by — must not be in the past | N | Date picker | — (blank) | Date is today or later | Per-item delivery date |
| **Comment** | Any additional notes for this line item — visible to purchasers and approvers | N | Text | — (blank) | — | Appears below the row |
| **Delete (×)** | Removes this item row from the grid — triggers a confirmation dialog before removing | — | Icon button | — | — | Shows "Remove Item" confirmation dialog; row removed only on Delete confirm |

---

## 5.5 Adding Items

Click **Add Item** to append a new blank inline row at the bottom of the grid. Fill all mandatory fields before saving. Multiple rows can be added consecutively.

---

## 5.6 Deleting Items

The trash icon (🗑) appears at the right of each editable item row. Clicking it triggers a **Remove Item confirmation dialog** before the row is removed.

**Edit mode — items table with trash icons:**

![Edit mode items table with trash icons](data:image/png;base64,)

**Remove Item confirmation dialog (full viewport):**

![Remove Item dialog — full viewport](data:image/png;base64,)

**Remove Item confirmation dialog (focused):**

![Remove Item dialog — focused](data:image/png;base64,)

| Element | Confirmed Content |
|---------|------------------|
| Dialog title | **Remove Item** |
| Body message | Are you sure you want to remove "[product name]"? |
| Product reference | Full product name shown in quotes |
| **Delete** button | Red/Destructive — labelled **Delete** |
| **Cancel** button | Outline/Grey — labelled **Cancel** |

On **Delete confirm**: the row is removed from the grid. The change is **not persisted** until Save is clicked — if the user Cancels (exits edit mode), the row is restored.

On **Cancel** (in dialog): the dialog closes; the item row remains in the grid unchanged.

> **Note:** The trash icon on APPROVED item rows (items that have been approved at a previous stage) is **disabled** — those rows cannot be deleted by the Creator.

---

## 5.7 Action Buttons, Behaviour & Validation

This section covers all buttons available when editing a Draft or Returned PR: their purpose, availability, and validation rules.

> **Form states:**
> - **New** (URL: `/purchase-request/new`) — before first save with items; no PR number yet
> - **Saved Draft** (URL: `/purchase-request/:uuid`) — after first save with ≥ 1 item

### Button Summary

| Button | Color | Available: New state | Available: Saved Draft | Purpose / Use Case |
|--------|-------|---------------------|------------------------|--------------------|
| **Cancel** | Outline/Grey | ✅ Always | ✅ Always | Abandon edits and return to PR List — use when you want to discard all unsaved changes |
| **Save** | Secondary | ✅ Always | ✅ Always | Save the current state as Draft so you can continue later — no submission required |
| **Delete** | Red/Danger | ❌ Not available | ✅ Saved Draft only | Permanently remove a Draft PR that is no longer needed |
| **Submit** | Blue/Primary | Only when ≥ 1 item + Workflow selected | Only when ≥ 1 item + Workflow selected | Send the completed PR for approval — triggers workflow routing |

---

### On Cancel

**Purpose / Use Case:** Return to the PR List, discarding any unsaved changes including added or deleted rows.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | Unsaved changes exist on the form | BR-03 | "Discard changes?" dialog shown (confirmed in UI) | If no changes: returns to PR List immediately |
| 2 | User selects Discard | — | All unsaved edits and deleted rows reverted; redirected to PR List | — |
| 3 | User selects Keep editing | — | Dialog closed; form returned to current state | — |

---

### On Save

**Purpose / Use Case:** Persist current progress as a Draft without submitting. Use when you need to pause, review prices, or are waiting on information before sending for approval.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | PR status is DRAFT or RETURNED | BR-01 | Save proceeds; stays on page | Save button not shown for other statuses |
| 2 | At least one item row is present | BR-03 | Items saved to database | Header saved only; URL stays at `/new` |
| 3 | Location is not empty (per row) | FR-PR-002 | Row saved | Location field highlighted as required |
| 4 | Product is not empty (per row) | FR-PR-002 | Unit and UoM auto-filled; row saved | Product field highlighted as required |
| 5 | Qty is more than 0 and at most 3 decimal places | BR-08 | Row saved; Amount auto-calculated | Row-level error shown on Qty field |
| 6 | Delivery date is today or later (if entered) | BR-10 | Date accepted | Error shown below date field |
| 7 | No concurrent edit lock by another user | BR-06 | Save succeeds | Server returns lock/conflict error (TBC) |
| 8 | All edits written to audit trail | BR-05 | Field-level change log appended | — (server-side; no UI feedback) |

> **Note:** Deleted rows are only committed to the database on Save. Until Save is clicked, a deleted row is restored if you cancel and discard.

---

### On Submit

**Purpose / Use Case:** Send the fully prepared PR for approval. Use only when all items and quantities are confirmed. Submission cannot be undone.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | Workflow (PR Type) is selected | BR-08 | Submit button is active | Button is disabled; no tooltip shown |
| 2 | At least 1 item row is present | BR-08 | Submit button is active | Button is disabled |
| 3 | Location is not empty on all rows | FR-PR-002 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on Location field |
| 4 | Product is not empty on all rows | FR-PR-002 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on Product field |
| 5 | Qty is more than 0 on all rows | FR-PR-002 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on Qty field |
| 6 | Budget is not exceeded | BR-09 | Soft commitment created; status → INPROGRESS | Warning shown or blocked depending on org config |
| 7 | PR status is still DRAFT or RETURNED at submit time | FR-PR-006 | Status → INPROGRESS; routed to first approver | Server rejects if status was changed by a concurrent action |
| 8 | Audit entry recorded | BR-05 | Workflow History updated with RECEIVED entry | — |

---

### On Delete

**Purpose / Use Case:** Permanently remove a Draft PR that is no longer needed — for example if it was created by mistake. Only available once a PR number has been assigned.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | PR status is DRAFT | BR-08 | "Confirm delete?" dialog shown | Delete button not visible for non-Draft status |
| 2 | Creator is the PR owner | FR-PR-005A | PR permanently deleted after confirm; redirected to PR List | Deletion rejected (TBC) |

---

## 5.8 Cancel — Discard Changes Dialog

![Discard Changes Dialog](data:image/png;base64,)

Triggered when Cancel is clicked and there are unsaved changes.

| Element | Content |
|---------|---------|
| Title | **Discard changes?** |
| Body | "You have unsaved changes that will be lost." |
| **Keep editing** | Outline button — closes dialog, returns to form |
| **Discard** | Red/destructive button — discards all unsaved changes, navigates to PR List |

> This dialog fulfils BRD FR-PR-011 requirement: "Warning message when navigating away with unsaved changes."

---

## 5.9 Business Rules

| # | Rule | Source |
|---|------|--------|
| BR-01 | Only **Draft** and **Returned** PRs are editable by the Creator. All other statuses are read-only. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-02 | Editing a Returned PR and re-submitting changes its status from Returned → INPROGRESS. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-03 | Item row delete is immediate in the UI but only committed to the database on **Save**. Navigating away without saving restores the deleted row. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-04 | Auto-save every 30 seconds once a Draft UUID exists (BRD spec). Not confirmed in current UI. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-05 | All edits are tracked in the audit trail with: field changed, old value, new value, user, timestamp. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-06 | Edit locking: if another user opens the same Draft for editing, it should show "locked by [user]" indicator. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-07 | PR Number is assigned on **first save** with items. While at `/new` with no saved state, no PR number exists. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-08 | Product selection auto-fills Unit of Measure and suggests last vendor price from history. | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-09 | Vendor suggestions (last 90 days) appear when a product is selected. | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-10 | Delivery date per item must be ≥ today; system should prevent past dates. | FR-PR-002 |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-04):** Auto-save every 30s is specified in BRD (FR-PR-011). The URL stays at `/new` even after multiple saves without items, suggesting auto-save is not active on the new form. Needs verification on a saved (:uuid) form.

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-06):** Edit locking / "locked by" indicator was not observed in the current UI. Concurrent edit protection is likely not yet implemented.

---

## 5.10 Edge Cases & Error States

| # | Scenario | System Behaviour |
|---|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Save clicked with all item fields blank | Saves header only; Submit remains disabled |
| 2 | Cancel clicked with no changes made | Returns to PR List immediately (no dialog) |
| 3 | Cancel clicked with unsaved changes | "Discard changes?" dialog with Keep editing / Discard |
| 4 | Delete row accidentally — then Cancel without saving | Row is restored (delete not persisted) |
| 5 | Product not found in search | Empty dropdown state; Creator cannot proceed with that row |
| 6 | Delivery date set to past date | TBC — expected validation error; behaviour not confirmed |
| 7 | Network error during Save | Error toast expected; form stays open (TBC) |
| 8 | PR transitions to INPROGRESS while Creator is editing (race condition) | Next Save should fail with permission error (TBC) |

---

## 5.11 Navigation

| Action | Destination |
|--------|-------------|
| **Cancel → Discard** | Step 1 — PR List |
| **Cancel → Keep editing** | Stays on edit form |
| **Save** | Stays on edit form at `:uuid` URL |
| **Submit → Confirm** | Step 4 — PR Detail (INPROGRESS, read-only) |
| **Delete → Confirm** | Step 1 — PR List |
| Breadcrumb **Purchase Request** | Step 1 — PR List |
